contentBlocks.original:
वीताखिल-विषयेच्छं जातानन्दाश्र पुलकमत्यच्छम् ।
सीतापति दूताद्यं वातात्मजमद्य भावये हृद्यम् ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vītākhila-viṣayecchaṃ jātānandāśra pulakamatyaccham .
sītāpati dūtādyaṃ vātātmajamadya bhāvaye hṛdyam .. 1..
contentBlocks.translation: I now call to mind Hanuman, the son of the wind god, gladdening to contemplate, who is free of all sensual desires, who sheds tears of joy and is filled with rapture, who is the purest of the pure and the first of Rama's messengers.
contentBlocks.meanings: वीताखिल-विषयेच्छम् (vītākhila-viṣayeccham) – free of all desires (vīta:free of | akhila:all | viṣaya:sensual objects | iccham:desire); जातानन्दाश्र (jātānandāśra) – tears of joy (jāta:born | ānanda:joy | aśra:tears); पुलकमत्यच्छम् (pulakamatyaccham) – filled with rapture (pulaka:rapture | mati:mind | accham:pure); सीतापति (sītāpati) – Lord of Sita (sītā:Sita | pati:lord); दूताद्यं (dūtādyaṃ) – first of messengers (dūta:messenger | ādyaṃ:first); वातात्मजम् (vātātmajam) – son of the wind god (vāta:wind | ātmaja:son); अद्य (adya) – now; भावये (bhāvaye) – I contemplate; हृद्यम् (hṛdyam) – gladdening;

contentBlocks.translation: I think of Hanuman, whose face is like the lotus, red like the rising sun, the corners of whose eyes are full of the feeling of mercy, who is life-giving, whose greatness has the quality of beauty, who personifies Anjana's good fortune.
contentBlocks.meanings: तरुणारुण (taruṇāruṇa) – young and red (taruṇa:young | aruṇa:red); मुख (mukha) – face; कमलं (kamalaṃ) – lotus; करुणा-रसपूर-पूरितापाङ्गम् (karuṇā-rasapūra-pūritāpāṅgam) – corners of eyes full of mercy (karuṇā:mercy | rasa:essence | pūra:full | pūrita:filled | apāṅgam:corners of eyes); सञ्जीवनम् (sañjīvanam) – life-giving; आशासे (āśāse) – I think of; मञ्जुल-महिमानम् (mañjula-mahimānam) – whose greatness has beauty (mañjula:beautiful | mahimānam:greatness); अञ्जना-भाग्यम् (añjanā-bhāgyam) – Anjana's good fortune (añjanā:Anjana | bhāgyam:good fortune);

contentBlocks.translation: I seek refuge in the one who flies faster than the arrows of madana, whose eyes wide as the petals of the lotus are filled with kindness, whose neck is smooth and well-formed as the conch shell, who represented good fortune to the wind god, and whose lips are bright-red like the bimba fruit.
contentBlocks.meanings: शम्बरवैरि-शरातिगम् (śambaravairi-śarātigam) – faster than Madana's arrows (śambara:Madana | vaira:enemy | śara:arrow | atigam:faster than); अम्बुजदल-विपुल-लोचन-उदारम् (ambujadala-vipula-locana-udāram) – eyes wide as lotus petals (ambuja:lotus | dala:petal | vipula:wide | locana:eyes | udāram:kind); कम्बुगलम् (kambugalam) – conch-like neck (kambu:conch | gala:neck); अनिलदिष्टम् (aniladiṣṭam) – good fortune to wind god (anila:wind | diṣṭam:fortune); बिम्ब-ज्वलित-ओष्ठम् (bimba-jvalita-oṣṭham) – lips bright-red like bimba (bimba:bimba fruit | jvalita:bright-red | oṣṭham:lips); एकम् (ekam) – one; अवलम्बे (avalambe) – I seek refuge;

contentBlocks.original:
दूरीकृत-सीतार्तिः प्रकटीकृत-रामवैभव-स्फूर्तिः ।
दारित-दशमुख-कीर्तिः पुरतो मम भातु हनुमतो मूर्तिः ॥ ४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dūrīkṛta-sītārtiḥ prakaṭīkṛta-rāmavaibhava-sphūrtiḥ .
dārita-daśamukha-kīrtiḥ purato mama bhātu hanumato mūrtiḥ .. 4..
contentBlocks.translation: May the form of Hanuman come resplendent before me, the one that dispelled Sita's grief, that brought out the glory of Shri Rama's prowess, that tore Ravana's reputation into shreds.
contentBlocks.meanings: दूरीकृत (dūrīkṛta) – dispelled; सीतार्तिः (sītārtiḥ) – Sita's grief (sītā:Sita | artiḥ:grief); प्रकटीकृत (prakaṭīkṛta) – made manifest; रामवैभवस्फूर्तिः (rāmavaibhavasphūrtiḥ) – glory of Rama's prowess (rāma:Rama | vaibhava:glory | sphūrtiḥ:prowess); दारित (dārita) – torn; दशमुखकीर्तिः (daśamukhakīrtiḥ) – Ravana's reputation (daśamukha:Ravana | kīrtiḥ:reputation); पुरतः (purataḥ) – before; मम (mama) – my; भातु (bhātu) – may it shine; हनुमतो (hanumato) – of Hanuman; मूर्तिः (mūrtiḥ) – form;

contentBlocks.original:
वानर-निकराध्यक्षं दानवकुल-कुमुद-रविकर-सदृशम् ।
दीन-जनावन-दीक्षं पवन तपः पाकपुञ्जमद्राक्षम् ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vānara-nikarādhyakṣaṃ dānavakula-kumuda-ravikara-sadṛśam .
dīna-janāvana-dīkṣaṃ pavana tapaḥ pākapuñjamadrākṣam .. 5..
contentBlocks.translation: I saw the leader of the Vanara (monkey) populace, the one who was (inimical) like the sun's rays to the (night-blooming) lily of the Danava people (the demonic race), who is dedicated to the protection of those in distress, who was the culmination of the accumulated penances of Vayu.
contentBlocks.meanings: वानर (vānara) – monkey; निकराध्यक्षं (nikarādhyakṣam) – leader of the group (nikara:group | adhyakṣam:leader); दानवकुल (dānavakula) – of the Danava race (dānava:Danava | kula:race); कुमुद (kumuda) – night-blooming lily; रविकर (ravikara) – sun's rays (ravi:sun | kara:rays); सदृशम् (sadṛśam) – like; दीन (dīna) – distressed; जनावन (janāvana) – protection of people (jana:people | avana:protection); दीक्षं (dīkṣam) – dedicated; पवन (pavana) – Vayu; तपः (tapaḥ) – penance; पाकपुञ्जम् (pākapuñjam) – culmination of penances (pāka:culmination | puñjam:penances); अद्राक्षम् (adrākṣam) – I saw;

contentBlocks.original:
एतत्-पवन-सुतस्य स्तोत्रं यः पठति पञ्चरत्नाख्यम् ।
चिरमिह-निखिलान् भोगान् भुङ्क्त्वा श्रीराम-भक्ति-भाग्-भवति ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
etat-pavana-sutasya stotraṃ yaḥ paṭhati pañcaratnākhyam .
ciramiha-nikhilān bhogān bhuṅktvā śrīrāma-bhakti-bhāg-bhavati .. 6..
contentBlocks.translation: He who recites this hymn to Hanuman, will become one with the devotees of Shri Rama after enjoying for long the pleasures of this world.
contentBlocks.meanings: एतत् (etat) – this; पवनसुतस्य (pavanasutasya) – of the son of the wind (pavana:wind | sutasya:of the son); स्तोत्रं (stotraṃ) – hymn; यः (yaḥ) – who; पठति (paṭhati) – recites; पञ्चरत्नाख्यम् (pañcaratnākhyam) – called five jewels (pañca:five | ratna:jewels | ākhyam:called); चिरम् (ciram) – for long; इह (iha) – here; निखिलान् (nikhilān) – all; भोगान् (bhogān) – pleasures; भुङ्क्त्वा (bhuṅktvā) – enjoying; श्रीरामभक्तिभाग् (śrīrāmabhaktibhāg) – one with devotees of Shri Rama (śrī:Shri | rāma:Rama | bhakti:devotion | bhāg:part); भवति (bhavati) – becomes;
